mission.title: Install and Configure Inclavare Containers on Kubernetes
mission.description: Inclavare Containers is a novel container runtime designed for confidential computing, providing hardware-enforced isolation for sensitive workloads. Installing Inclavare Containers allows you to run protected containers in a Trusted Execution Environment, ensuring your data remains secure from untrusted entities.

steps.title: Create Namespace
steps.description: Create a dedicated namespace for Inclavare Containers to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Deploy Inclavare Containers
steps.description: Apply the necessary manifests to deploy Inclavare Containers.

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description: Check if the Inclavare Containers pods are running successfully.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Inclavare Containers will show the pods running in the 'inclavare' namespace. You should also see the resource limits applied and the RBAC permissions set correctly.

uninstall.title: Remove Inclavare Containers
uninstall.description: Delete the deployed resources and the namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Inclavare Containers
upgrade.description: Reapply the manifests to upgrade to the latest stable version.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Running
troubleshooting.description: If the pods are not in a Running state, check the pod logs for errors using: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n inclavare-containers`.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: Ensure your cluster has enough resources available. Check node status with: `kubectl get nodes`.

troubleshooting.title: Network Issues
troubleshooting.description: If you cannot access the service, verify the service configuration and check network policies that might be blocking access.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with it.
